revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use}} {{Template shortcut|flagdeco}} Displays the flag identified by the parameter in "icon" size, currently 22x20 pixels plus a one pixel border. This template is very similar to {{tl|flag icon}}, except the image is not linked to an article, i.e. it is "purely decorative" per [[World Wide Web Consortium]] (W3C) [[Web Content Accessibility Guidelines]] (WCAG). This template can be used when the article text immediately adjacent to the icon provides the associated link for the flag. ;Comparison example: *{{tlx|flag icon|United Kingdom}} → {{flag icon|United Kingdom}} *{{tlx|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} ;Usage examples: *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[Portugal]] *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] As with {{tlf|flag icon}}, a particular variant of the flag and/or a custom size for the icon may be specified using (respectively) {{para|variant}} and/or {{para|size}}. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. Also as with {{tlf|flag icon}}, the display of a redlink when Template:Country data Foo does not exist can be suppressed by using the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. == Tracking categories == Where {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Foo}} is used, but the Template:Country data Foo does not exist, then a [[WP:REDLINK|redlink]] is displayed, ''unless'' the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter has been set. Where the redlink is displayed, the page is addded to a tracking category: * [[:Category:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates}}) == See also == * [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] * [[Wikipedia:Alternative text for images]] {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use}} {{Template shortcut|flagdeco}} Displays the flag identified by the parameter in "icon" size, currently 22x20 pixels plus a one pixel border. This template is very similar to {{tl|flag icon}}, except the image is not linked to an article, i.e. it is "purely decorative" per [[World Wide Web Consortium]] (W3C) [[Web Content Accessibility Guidelines]] (WCAG). This template can be used when the article text immediately adjacent to the icon provides the associated link for the flag. ;Comparison example: *{{tlx|flag icon|United Kingdom}} → {{flag icon|United Kingdom}} *{{tlx|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} ;Usage examples: *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] As with {{tlf|flag icon}}, a particular variant of the flag and/or a custom size for the icon may be specified using (respectively) {{para|variant}} and/or {{para|size}}. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. Also as with {{tlf|flag icon}}, the display of a redlink when Template:Country data Foo does not exist can be suppressed by using the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. == Tracking categories == Where {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Foo}} is used, but the Template:Country data Foo does not exist, then a [[WP:REDLINK|redlink]] is displayed, ''unless'' the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter has been set. Where the redlink is displayed, the page is addded to a tracking category: * [[:Category:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates}}) == See also == * [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] * [[Wikipedia:Alternative text for images]] {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use}} {{Template shortcut|flagdeco}} Displays the flag identified by the parameter in "icon" size, currently 22x20 pixels plus a one pixel border. This template is very similar to {{tl|flag icon}}, except the image is not linked to an article, i.e. it is "purely decorative" per [[World Wide Web Consortium]] (W3C) [[:en:Web Content Accessibility Guidelines|Web Content Accessibility Guidelines]] (WCAG). This template can be used when the article text immediately adjacent to the icon provides the associated link for the flag. ;Comparison example: *{{tlx|flag icon|United Kingdom}} → {{flag icon|United Kingdom}} *{{tlx|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} ;Usage examples: *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] As with {{tlf|flag icon}}, a particular variant of the flag and/or a custom size for the icon may be specified using (respectively) {{para|variant}} and/or {{para|size}}. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. Also as with {{tlf|flag icon}}, the display of a redlink when Template:Country data Foo does not exist can be suppressed by using the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. == Tracking categories == Where {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Foo}} is used, but the Template:Country data Foo does not exist, then a [[WP:REDLINK|redlink]] is displayed, ''unless'' the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter has been set. Where the redlink is displayed, the page is addded to a tracking category: * [[:Category:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates}}) == See also == * [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] * [[Wikipedia:Alternative text for images]] {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use}} {{Template shortcut|flagdeco}} Displays the flag identified by the parameter in "icon" size, currently 22x20 pixels plus a one pixel border. This template is very similar to {{tl|flag icon}}, except the image is not linked to an article, i.e. it is "purely decorative" per [[:en:World Wide Web Consortium|World Wide Web Consortium]] (W3C) [[:en:Web Content Accessibility Guidelines|Web Content Accessibility Guidelines]] (WCAG). This template can be used when the article text immediately adjacent to the icon provides the associated link for the flag. ;Comparison example: *{{tlx|flag icon|United Kingdom}} → {{flag icon|United Kingdom}} *{{tlx|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} ;Usage examples: *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] As with {{tlf|flag icon}}, a particular variant of the flag and/or a custom size for the icon may be specified using (respectively) {{para|variant}} and/or {{para|size}}. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. Also as with {{tlf|flag icon}}, the display of a redlink when Template:Country data Foo does not exist can be suppressed by using the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. == Tracking categories == Where {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Foo}} is used, but the Template:Country data Foo does not exist, then a [[WP:REDLINK|redlink]] is displayed, ''unless'' the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter has been set. Where the redlink is displayed, the page is addded to a tracking category: * [[:Category:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates}}) == See also == * [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] * [[Wikipedia:Alternative text for images]] {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use}} {{Template shortcut|flagdeco}} Displays the flag identified by the parameter in "icon" size, currently 22x20 pixels plus a one pixel border. This template is very similar to {{tl|flag icon}}, except the image is not linked to an article, i.e. it is "purely decorative" per [[:en:World Wide Web Consortium|World Wide Web Consortium]] (W3C) [[:en:Web Content Accessibility Guidelines|Web Content Accessibility Guidelines]] (WCAG). This template can be used when the article text immediately adjacent to the icon provides the associated link for the flag. ;Comparison example: *{{tlx|flag icon|United Kingdom}} → {{flag icon|United Kingdom}} *{{tlx|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} ;Usage examples: *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] As with {{tlf|flag icon}}, a particular variant of the flag and/or a custom size for the icon may be specified using (respectively) {{para|variant}} and/or {{para|size}}. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. Also as with {{tlf|flag icon}}, the display of a redlink when Template:Country data Foo does not exist can be suppressed by using the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. == Tracking categories == Where {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Foo}} is used, but the Template:Country data Foo does not exist, then a [[WP:REDLINK|redlink]] is displayed, ''unless'' the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter has been set. Where the redlink is displayed, the page is addded to a tracking category: * [[:Category:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates}}) == See also == * [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] * [[Wikipedia:Alternative text for images]] {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use}} {{Template shortcut|flagdeco}} Displays the flag identified by the parameter in "icon" size, currently 22x20 pixels plus a one pixel border. This template is very similar to {{tl|flag icon}}, except the image is not linked to an article, i.e. it is "purely decorative" per [[:en:World Wide Web Consortium|World Wide Web Consortium]] (W3C) [[:en:Web Content Accessibility Guidelines|Web Content Accessibility Guidelines]] (WCAG). This template can be used when the article text immediately adjacent to the icon provides the associated link for the flag. ;Comparison example: *{{tlx|flag icon|United Kingdom}} → {{flag icon|United Kingdom}} *{{tlx|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} ;Usage examples: *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] As with {{tlf|flag icon}}, a particular variant of the flag and/or a custom size for the icon may be specified using (respectively) {{para|variant}} and/or {{para|size}}. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. Also as with {{tlf|flag icon}}, the display of a redlink when Template:Country data Foo does not exist can be suppressed by using the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. == Tracking categories == Where {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Foo}} is used, but the Template:Country data Foo does not exist, then a [[WP:REDLINK|redlink]] is displayed, ''unless'' the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter has been set. Where the redlink is displayed, the page is addded to a tracking category: * [[:Category:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates}}) == See also == * [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] * [[Wikipedia:Alternative text for images]] {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use}} {{Template shortcut|flagdeco}} Displays the flag identified by the parameter in "icon" size, currently 22x20 pixels plus a one pixel border. This template is very similar to {{tl|flag icon}}, except the image is not linked to an article, i.e. it is "purely decorative" per [[:en:World Wide Web Consortium|World Wide Web Consortium]] (W3C) [[:en:Web Content Accessibility Guidelines|Web Content Accessibility Guidelines]] (WCAG). This template can be used when the article text immediately adjacent to the icon provides the associated link for the flag. ;Comparison example: *{{tlx|flag icon|United Kingdom}} → {{flag icon|United Kingdom}} *{{tlx|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} ;Usage examples: *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] As with {{tlf|flag icon}}, a particular variant of the flag and/or a custom size for the icon may be specified using (respectively) {{para|variant}} and/or {{para|size}}. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. Also as with {{tlf|flag icon}}, the display of a redlink when Template:Country data Foo does not exist can be suppressed by using the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. == Tracking categories == Where {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Foo}} is used, but the Template:Country data Foo does not exist, then a [[WP:REDLINK|redlink]] is displayed, ''unless'' the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter has been set. Where the redlink is displayed, the page is addded to a tracking category: * [[:Category:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates}}) == See also == * [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] * [[Wikipedia:Alternative text for images]] {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23410180721|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use}} {{Template shortcut|flagdeco}} Displays the flag identified by the parameter in "icon" size, currently 22x20 pixels plus a one pixel border. This template is very similar to {{tl|flag icon}}, except the image is not linked to an article, i.e. it is "purely decorative" per [[:en:World Wide Web Consortium|World Wide Web Consortium]] (W3C) [[:en:Web Content Accessibility Guidelines|Web Content Accessibility Guidelines]] (WCAG). This template can be used when the article text immediately adjacent to the icon provides the associated link for the flag. ;Comparison example: *{{tlx|flag icon|United Kingdom}} → {{flag icon|United Kingdom}} *{{tlx|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} ;Usage examples: *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] As with {{tlf|flag icon}}, a particular variant of the flag and/or a custom size for the icon may be specified using (respectively) {{para|variant}} and/or {{para|size}}. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. Also as with {{tlf|flag icon}}, the display of a redlink when Template:Country data Foo does not exist can be suppressed by using the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. == Tracking categories == Where {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Foo}} is used, but the Template:Country data Foo does not exist, then a [[WP:REDLINK|redlink]] is displayed, ''unless'' the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter has been set. Where the redlink is displayed, the page is addded to a tracking category: * [[:Category:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates}}) == See also == * [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] * [[Wikipedia:Alternative text for images]] {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Flag_decoration%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use}} {{Template shortcut|flagdeco}} Displays the flag identified by the parameter in "icon" size, currently 22x20 pixels plus a one pixel border. This template is very similar to {{tl|flag icon}}, except the image is not linked to an article, i.e. it is "purely decorative" per [[:en:World Wide Web Consortium|World Wide Web Consortium]] (W3C) [[:en:Web Content Accessibility Guidelines|Web Content Accessibility Guidelines]] (WCAG). This template can be used when the article text immediately adjacent to the icon provides the associated link for the flag. ;Comparison example: *{{tlx|flag icon|United Kingdom}} → {{flag icon|United Kingdom}} *{{tlx|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|United Kingdom}} ;Usage examples: *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Lisboa]], [[:en:Portugal|Portugal]] *{{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] → {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Portugal}} [[Portuguese First Republic|Portugal]] As with {{tlf|flag icon}}, a particular variant of the flag and/or a custom size for the icon may be specified using (respectively) {{para|variant}} and/or {{para|size}}. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. Also as with {{tlf|flag icon}}, the display of a redlink when Template:Country data Foo does not exist can be suppressed by using the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter. See {{tl|flag icon}} for further details. == Tracking categories == Where {{{{BASEPAGENAME}}|Foo}} is used, but the Template:Country data Foo does not exist, then a [[WP:REDLINK|redlink]] is displayed, ''unless'' the {{para|noredlink|yes}} parameter has been set. Where the redlink is displayed, the page is addded to a tracking category: * [[:Category:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:{{BASEPAGENAME}} missing country data templates}}) == See also == * [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] * [[Wikipedia:Alternative text for images]] {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
